rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component type scope when the contract relates to component type features
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only inserting annex blocks, not modifying component structure
Use visible identifiers from the target component's features when writing AGREE expressions
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annex blocks
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by only inserting annex blocks, not modifying component structure
Use visible identifiers from the target component's features when writing AGREE expressions
Preserve existing AADL properties and connections when inserting annex blocks
Place implementation-specific bindings in implementation annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `const` for constant declarations
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annex blocks
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annex blocks
Do not use component type ports in implementation annex blocks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement for assumptions and guarantees
